title: WildFly JMS Queue Depth and Consumer Lag
app: Splunk Universal Forwarder; Splunk Add-on for JMX (`Splunk_TA_jmx`); file monitoring for `jboss:server`, structured audit for `wildfly:audit`
spl:
index=web sourcetype="jmx:jboss"
| search mbean="*jms*queue*" OR mbean="*messaging*server*"
| eval depth=tonumber(MessageCount) cons=tonumber(ConsumerCount)
| where depth > 1000 OR (depth > 0 AND cons == 0)
| table _time, host, mbean, depth, cons
description: Deep JMS queues mean producers outpace consumers or consumers failed silently. Zero consumers with positive depth is especially dangerous.
value: Prevents message loss scenarios and disk-full events on brokers embedded in WildFly.
implementation: Enable messaging statistics on queues. Poll JMX every minute; alert on rising `MessageCount` with zero consumers.
visualization: Time series for pool/queue metrics, top stats for failures, event timeline for deploys.
grandmaExplanation: We monitor message queues so backlog does not silently exhaust disk and memory.
